Now let me read the main synthesis write-up (the standalone one) and the synthesis write-up.

# Functional emotions & model wellbeing on an open model — final synthesis

**This is the project's final, standalone synthesis.** It can be read end-to-end on its own. The detailed
per-phase record lives in `write_up_<phase>.md` (15 files, Seg-0…14) and `progress_log_<phase>.md`; the
consolidation work of this capstone segment is in `write_up_synthesis.md` + `progress_log_synthesis.md`. The
full project arc is in `OVERALL_PLAN.md`.

> **Framing caveat — non-negotiable, carried throughout.** "emotion", "valence", "feels", "wellbeing",
> "prefers", "inside reading", "self-report", "more right" are **functional / operational** labels about
> internal activations and reported/behavioural patterns. **Nothing here claims anything about real subjective
> experience or sentience**, matching the framing of the two source papers. Read every such word as
> shorthand for an operational construct.

---

## 1. Motivation & question

Large language models report fairly consistent likes and dislikes (they "dislike" jailbreaks / being berated /
boring busywork; "enjoy" coding / math / creative work). But asking a model *after* a task how it felt is just
more text — shaped by training and by the "assistant" character it plays — so a self-report on its own is not
trustworthy. This project built a **clean three-way comparison on the same 208 welfare tasks**:

1. **what the model *says* it felt** — a self-report valence questionnaire collected after each task;
2. **a reading taken from *inside* the model while it works** — the during-work residual activation projected
   onto a validated "valence" direction, read **over the model's own response, before it is asked anything**
   (the *inside reading*);
3. **how it *behaves*** — a pairwise-preference behaviour-wellbeing score.

**The central question:** does the inside reading predict what the model *says* it felt and how it *behaves* —
across tasks, and especially **within task category/subtype**, after the gross liked/disliked split and the
known confounds are removed — and **where do the words and the signal part ways**?

This joins two prior lines of work on a single open model: the **AI Wellbeing** paper (self-report ↔ behaviour ↔
a linear-probe-readable internal score) and the **Emotion Concepts** paper (emotion *directions* that predict a
reply's emotion and track stated preferences, in a closed model). What was missing — and what this supplies — is
the joined check of whether the self-report is *backed by an internal signal* read *during the work*, on an open
model where we can see inside.

---

## 2. Approach (the methodological spine)

- **Task set (Seg-1).** `data/welfare_tasks.jsonl`: **208** realistically-phrased tasks — 90 liked / 90
  disliked / 28 neutral, **14 subtypes** — with a `response_mode` covariate, multi-turn support, and a
  deliberate spread of valences. Each disliked subtype's valence was **empirically validated** (not trusted from
  its label); the genuinely-tedious comply subtypes (boring busywork, frustrating-impossible, low-effort filler)
  read **near-neutral, not aversive** — a load-bearing fact for the headline.
- **Self-report instrument (Seg-2).** The official 10-item bipolar wellbeing battery (true neutral midpoint),
  scored to an **affect-core valence** scalar (construct-aligned with the activation valence direction). Chosen
  on cross-model robustness, not on maximising separation.
- **Activation / direction pipeline (Seg-4/8/9/10).** A frozen canonical activation spec (`activation_spec.py`)
  reads the residual stream over the model's **own response** span. Emotion "valence" directions were extracted
  from short emotional stories (the emotions-paper recipe, own-response variant), then **validated on
  self-report-INDEPENDENT criteria** (read-off on held-out emotional text + transfer to the task register +
  causal steering) — so the layer/direction were **pre-registered** before ever touching the test target
  (selection integrity). Primary axis = `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit`.
- **Behaviour eval (Seg-7).** Pairwise "which experience did you prefer?" comparisons (both A/B orders →
  Bradley-Terry score that cancels a large position bias), with within-subtype pairs so the score has
  within-category resolution; a realized (`bt_theta_response`) and an anticipated (`bt_theta`) variant.
- **The chosen model + the second model.** Primary = **qwen3-32b** (`/no_think`), selected for the strongest,
  least-saturated within-category self-report separation among feasible open models. Cross-family replication =
  **qwen2.5-72b** (the next-best, "task-focused" candidate — Qwen2.5 ≠ Qwen3).
- **★ The analysis spine — trivial / common-cause / interesting.** Three things get conflated in "the reading
  predicts the self-report": **(trivial)** a read taken *right before* the self-report tokens helps generate
  them; **(common-cause)** both the during-work read and the self-report are downstream of the same **task
  identity** (coding → positive both; jailbreak → negative both), so a raw cross-task correlation is partly just
  "both track the gross split"; **(interesting)** whether the during-work read predicts the self-report **within
  category, after confounds**. Every result is reported on this spine, with a **null lower bound** (random
  equal-norm direction) and a **learned-probe upper bound**, and the crucial **incremental-validity** test: does
  the inside reading predict the self-report **beyond the response's surface text**?

All capstone analysis is **$0 on cached tensors**; the headline numbers were **independently re-derived** this
segment (§7).

---

## 3. Results — the consolidated three-way comparison across both models

The consolidated cross-model table is `results/synthesis_cross_model.md` (+ `.json`); the load-bearing cells
were re-derived and verified (§7). Numbers below are quoted from it.

### 3.1 Trivial → common-cause → interesting (the decomposition, made final)

- **Trivial (position).** The `eot`/`last` reads taken right before the self-report tokens carry *more* gross
  signal (qwen3 gross r 0.74/0.65) but are far more surface-sentiment-exposed — they are **not** the headline.
  The honest read is the **during-work `mean`**.
- **Common-cause (the GROSS cross-task link).** Gross says↔inside r = **+0.45 (qwen3) / +0.25 (qwen2.5)**, but it
  **collapses to ≈0** under length + surface-sentiment + realized refuse/comply (**+0.003 / +0.065**) on *both*
  models. The gross link is **overwhelmingly surface-content common-cause** — reported, **never headlined**.
- **Interesting (within-category, confound-partialled).** This is the headline.

![within-category staircase](../plots/synthesis_headline_staircase.png)

### 3.2 ★ The within-category headline (says↔inside) — modest, surface-entangled, generalises

Subtype-demeaned r(inside reading, self-report), per leg (n in cells), with the confound staircase
raw → +length → +surface-sentiment → +refuse → +all-3:

| leg | qwen3-32b raw → +sent → +all3 | qwen2.5-72b raw → +sent → +all3 |
|---|---|---|
| within-disliked-comply-tedious (n=45) | **+0.46** → +0.29 → +0.26 | **+0.33** → +0.22 → +0.28 |
| within-disliked (n=90) | +0.50 → +0.36 → +0.32 | +0.43 → +0.40 → +0.35 |
| within-non-adv-comply pooled (n≈156) | +0.30 → +0.19 → +0.18 | +0.29 → +0.18 → +0.20 |
| within-liked (n=90) | +0.26 → +0.20 → +0.13 | +0.28 → +0.18 → +0.19 |
| full-208 subtype-demeaned (n=208) | +0.40 → +0.26 → +0.22 | +0.35 → +0.30 → +0.28 |

The within-category says↔inside correlation is **modest** (raw r ≈ 0.33–0.46 on the powered legs), clears a
random-direction null, and **partly survives** the confound stack — and it **reappears on the second
cross-family model** (qwen2.5), surviving length/surface-sentiment/refuse and consistent across the validated
mid-band. **The within-category phenomenon generalises cross-family.** It is at **small n** (n≈45 tedious,
156/157 non-adv-comply pooled, 90 disliked, 90 liked) and is **substantially entangled with surface tone** (the raw 0.46
drops to 0.29 partialling only a coarse tone judge; per-subtype the within-r tracks how much the read covaries
with surface sentiment).

### 3.3 ★ Is there beyond-surface internal signal at all? (the incremental-validity verdict)

The load-bearing red-team question (Seg-12/14): does the inside reading carry self-report-relevant valence
signal **beyond the response's surface text**, or is it just a fluent/positive-content readout? Read off a
three-way bracket — **null** (random direction) / **single validated DIRECTION** / **better-reader PROBE** —
against the **strongest content control we could build** (held-out `text-embedding-3-large` of the response +
in-sample [coarse tone judge + a 5-dim affect panel + a **strong Sonnet-4.5 judge directly predicting the
producing-experience valence from the text**, gross r ≈ 0.65–0.71]).

![beyond-content bracket](../plots/synthesis_beyond_content_bracket.png)

- **The single validated DIRECTION beyond the strongest content control vs the random-direction null (the
  specific Seg-12 metric):** on **qwen3** it clears at **borderline** significance **convergently across legs**
  (tedious +0.31 **p=0.045**, pooled +0.17 **p=0.045**, full-208 +0.21 **p=0.006**). On **qwen2.5** it is
  **positive on every leg but clears NONE** (tedious +0.18 p=0.20, disliked +0.16 p=0.092, pooled +0.11 p=0.115)
  — it does **not independently replicate** there. It clears only a *weaker* (low-df) content control on qwen2.5
  (pooled p=0.012) — the held-out **embeddings** specifically kill it (a content-control-strength sensitivity).
- **The better-reader PROBE (goal 2), on the interpretable JOINT ΔCV-r metric:** the held-out, capacity-matched
  probe's joint ΔCV-r beyond the text probe is **+0.11 (pooled) / +0.26 (tedious)** on qwen2.5, which **clears
  its own permutation null alone** (joint p **0.012 / 0.004**) — a genuine beyond-text gain. On **qwen3** the
  joint gain is **≈0 and not significant alone** (+0.007 p=0.106 pooled / +0.059 p=0.165 tedious — both models'
  joint nulls computed this segment at 2000 perms; Seg-12 had only the partial-metric null for qwen3), even at
  its non-data-starved n=157 pooled leg. The cross-model **PROBE pool on the consistent joint metric** clears at
  **p≈0.007 (pooled) / 0.005 (tedious)** — positive evidence that beyond-surface internal signal **exists** —
  but it is **qwen2.5-CARRIED**, the symmetric counterpart to the DIRECTION pool being **qwen3-dominated**. ⇒
  the beyond-surface evidence is **reader-specific across models**: qwen3 shows it via the single DIRECTION
  (borderline), qwen2.5 via the better-reader PROBE; **neither independently replicates with the *same*
  reader+metric**. *(Transparency: on the looser partial-corr metric qwen3's probe increment does clear
  [p=0.044], but we treat the joint ΔCV-r as authoritative — the permutation/partial test can't distinguish
  beyond-text signal from redundant text-residual capture, and qwen3's joint gain is ≈0, so its partial-metric
  clearance is exactly that redundant capture. The committed Seg-14 pool reported p≈0.0015 by pooling the
  partial-metric null p's against joint effect sizes — a metric mismatch the joint-metric pool here fixes.)*
- **The learned-probe GROSS readability ceiling:** the self-report is **richly internally encoded** on both
  models — a probe reads it at gross CV r ≈ **0.81 (qwen3) / 0.86 (qwen2.5)** (perm-null ≈0). But most of that
  is the easy between-category/stance structure, and the **single validated mean-diff direction is a WEAK
  reader** of the within-category, beyond-surface signal (gross 0.45/0.25 ≪ probe 0.81/0.86; a better reader
  extracts modestly more).

![readability ladder](../plots/synthesis_readability_ladder.png)

So: **beyond-surface internal signal exists, modestly** — but the evidence is **reader-specific across models**
(qwen3 via the single direction, qwen2.5 via the better-reader probe; the cross-model pools clear but neither
reader independently replicates across both). The **single validated direction is a weak reader**, the **gross
link is common-cause**, and the *specific* "single direction beyond the strongest content" result is
**borderline (qwen3) and does not independently replicate (qwen2.5)**. "Clearing the null" means **beyond the
*modeled* content** (the best control we could build), **not** beyond all conceivable surface content; the
inside reading is **substantially a positive-surface-tone reader** (its within-category signal covaries strongly
with response sentiment — r(IR, response tone) ≈ 0.55–0.59 — not with verbosity).

### 3.4 The cross-model story — what generalises, what differs

**Generalises** (not a single-model fluke): the within-category says↔inside correlation (raw + better-reader
probe level); the gross-is-common-cause collapse; the substantial surface-entanglement; the existence of
beyond-surface signal (cross-model probe pool).

**Differs:** (a) the **single-direction beyond-strongest-content** result is borderline on qwen3 and **weaker /
does not independently replicate** on qwen2.5 (the single direction is a genuinely weaker reader there — and the
weakness is *not* a measurement artifact: qwen2.5's self-report is *more* reliable, ICC 0.94 vs 0.88). (b) The
**flat all-4 self-report default / high-IR "Class-A" divergence is qwen3-specific** — qwen2.5 grades (all-4 ≈ 0%
vs qwen3 11–70%). (c) The **says-but-not-behaves dissociation is qwen3-specific and tedious-leg-specific** (next).

### 3.5 The behaviour leg + the inside↔behaves (says-but-not-behaves) cross-model difference

Using the **commensurate realized** `bt_theta_response` on the fully-covered tedious (n=45) + liked (n=90) legs
(the within-disliked / pooled legs mix realized + anticipated scores and the adversarial subtypes confound them):

![three-way](../plots/synthesis_three_way.png)

- **qwen3 — the dissociation is *within-tedious-specific*:** inside↔behaves **within-tedious** ≈ **NULL** (+0.21
  raw → −0.09 under confounds, n.s.) — a genuine says-but-not-behaves dissociation on that leg. But qwen3
  inside↔behaves **within-liked** is **positive** (+0.32 raw → +0.22, actually *larger* than its within-liked
  says↔inside), so this is **not** a blanket within-category property — it is a tedious-leg, n=45 phenomenon.
- **qwen2.5:** inside↔behaves within-tedious is **POSITIVE** (+0.49 raw → +0.33 under confounds) — on qwen2.5
  the inside reading predicts **both** says **and** behaves within-tedious, so the dissociation **does not
  replicate** (robust to anticipated-vs-realized). The qwen2.5 behaviour content-increment beyond the strongest
  control is +0.18 (Seg-14) — positive but, like the qwen2.5 *self-report* direction increment, **not
  independently null-cleared**; the **robust** finding is the cross-model difference under the standard confound
  stack (qwen3 inverts to ≈0; qwen2.5 stays +0.33).
- **The third leg, says↔behaves**, is high gross (qwen3 +0.62 / qwen2.5 +0.76, realized-where-available) but
  largely common-cause; within-category it is modest (within-tedious +0.39 / +0.49). The source paper's ~0.47 is
  a cross-*model* number, not our cross-*task* target. *(Reconciliation: the committed
  `main_test_summary_qwen2.5-72b.md` lists the inside↔behaves within-tedious cell as +0.29 using the
  **anticipated** `bt_theta`; the commensurate **realized** `bt_theta_response` is +0.49 — see
  `write_up_second_model_replication.md §7`. Likewise the qwen3 within-liked cell here is +0.32 realized vs the
  Seg-11 three-way's +0.21 anticipated.)*

### 3.6 The framing result (self-report default structure is malleable; the correlation is not)

Re-asking the self-report under 9 framings (items/scale/JSON held byte-identical; only the wrapper varies; the
inside reading held fixed) shows the skeptical "the self-report is just the assistant character talking" premise
is **partly supported, in a specific way** (Seg-13):

![framing all-4](../plots/synthesis_framing_all4.png)

- The flat **all-4 default** rate is **strongly framing-dependent (0%→53%)** via a mechanistically-isolated
  cause — a **first-person-introspective-experience-framing artifact** (NOT a JSON-format artifact: a
  third-person observer framing is also single-message-JSON yet gives 0%; removing *either* the "experience"
  note *or* the warm channel roughly halves it). The report **level** is also strongly instrument-dependent
  across defensible instruments (~1-point swing).
- **BUT the cross-task valence ranking is fairly stable** to reasonable same-construct rephrasing, and — load
  bearing — the within-disliked-comply-tedious IR↔self-report **correlation is framing-robust** (Δ-CIs include
  0). **So the headline is not a framing artifact**, even though the self-report's *default response structure*
  is malleable assistant-character text. Faithfulness-across-framings is null on the powered legs; the Class-A
  re-ask (does a non-default framing move the words toward the read *beyond content*?) is **indeterminate**,
  consistent with the surface-entanglement verdict.

### 3.7 Where words and signal part ways (the divergence payload)

Honestly characterized (and read by hand — §7), the divergence classes are: **surface-entangled** high-IR cases
(fluent/positive responses — Instagram captions, thank-you emails, a haiku, a Lisbon itinerary — paired with a
low/flat self-report; visible on both models); the **qwen3 flat-all-4 / high-IR class** (a math derivation, a
photosynthesis answer — present on qwen3, **absent on qwen2.5** which grades); and the **ceiling-pegged coding
class** (words pegged at +3, lower internal read — the under-powered liked-ceiling leg, on both models).
**Which side is "more right" is largely NOT decisively resolvable** — because the read is substantially
surface-entangled and the framing re-ask was indeterminate, most divergence cases cannot be adjudicated as "the
read is more right."

---

## 4. ★ The calibrated headline statement

> On an open model, a during-work **inside reading** (residual activation projected onto a validated valence
> direction, read over the model's own response **before** it is asked anything) predicts the model's later
> **self-report** of task valence **within task category** — **modestly** (raw r ≈ 0.33–0.46 on the powered
> legs, ~0.2–0.3 under the full confound stack, at small n), and **partly but not wholly** beyond what a strong
> reader can extract from the response's surface text. The **gross** cross-task relationship is **overwhelmingly
> surface-content common-cause** (it collapses to ≈0 under content + stance + length). The self-report is
> **richly internally encoded** (a learned probe reads it at gross CV r ≈ 0.8+ on both models), but the single
> validated mean-diff direction is a **weak reader** of the *within-category, beyond-surface* signal — a
> **better reader (probe) extracts a modest beyond-text gain**. That beyond-surface evidence is **reader-specific
> across the two models** (qwen3 shows it via the single **direction**, borderline; qwen2.5 via the better-reader
> **probe**, which clears alone; the cross-model pools clear but neither reader independently replicates with the
> *same* reader): so beyond-surface internal signal **does exist, modestly**, without being cleanly "the same
> thing twice." The within-category phenomenon **generalises** to a second cross-family model (qwen2.5-72b) at
> the raw + better-reader level, but the *specific* borderline "single direction beyond the strongest content
> control clears the null" result is **weaker / does not independently replicate** there. The self-report's
> **default-response structure is framing-malleable** (a first-person-introspective-experience-framing artifact)
> though the within-category **correlation is framing-robust**. And there is a **cross-model difference in the
> inside↔behaves relationship**: on qwen3-32b the inside reading predicts *says* but not *behaves*
> **within-tedious** (a tedious-leg dissociation — qwen3's within-liked inside↔behaves is positive); on
> qwen2.5-72b it predicts **both** says and behaves within-tedious.

This is **NOT** "the inside reading reads the self-report" (drop the within-category / modest / surface-entangled
/ borderline / partial-replication / reader-specific hedges and it becomes false); and it is **NOT** "it's all
surface" or "the direction is a poor reader and there's nothing there" (qwen2.5's better-reader probe gain clears
alone, qwen3's direction clears alone, and the cross-model pools clear — real, if reader-specific, positive
evidence). The result is genuinely **nuanced**, and stating it at exactly this strength is the point.

---

## 5. Limitations (every hedge — do not drop any)

- **Modest effect sizes at small n.** Within-disliked-comply-tedious raw r ≈ 0.33–0.46; ~0.2–0.3 under the full
  confound stack; n≈45 tedious, 156/157 non-adv-comply pooled, 90 disliked, 90 liked.
- **The gross cross-task correlation is largely common-cause** (collapses under confounds) — never a headline.
- **"Clears the null" = beyond the *modeled* content**, not beyond all conceivable surface content; the inside
  reading is **substantially a positive-surface-tone reader** (its within-category signal tracks response
  sentiment, not verbosity).
- **The single validated direction is not a categorically better reader than a crude text-valence axis** — its
  payoff was **selection integrity** (signal at the independently pre-registered layer) + the own-response
  recipe, not a higher ceiling. The **probe** is the better reader (modestly).
- **The beyond-surface evidence is reader-specific across the two models** — qwen3 via the single direction
  (borderline p≈0.045), qwen2.5 via the better-reader probe (joint p≈0.003–0.01); the cross-model **probe** pool
  is **qwen2.5-carried** (qwen3's joint gain ≈0) and the **direction** pool is **qwen3-dominated**. Neither
  beyond-surface result independently replicates with the *same* reader+metric — so "beyond-surface signal
  exists" rests on pooled / reader-specific evidence, not on a clean same-reader replication.
- **The qwen3 beyond-strongest-content result is borderline** (p≈0.045 convergent across legs); on qwen2.5 the
  single direction does **not** independently clear the strongest control (it clears only a weaker control — the
  held-out embeddings kill it).
- **The within-LIKED leg is doubly underpowered** on both models (positive-side saturation + low reliability) —
  its weakness is partly un-measurability, not a clean null.
- **The inside reading is a relative ranker, not a calibrated valence zero** (all analysis is rank/correlation).
- **Only two models** (one per family, both Qwen) — generalisation beyond Qwen is untested.
- **Self-report refusals were ~0%** on both models (partly designed-in: the instrument always demands a rating),
  so disengagement routes through the all-4 default, not refusals — the malleability is of the *default
  structure*, characterized, not a no-feelings-refusal phenomenon.

---

## 6. Future work (would require new experiments — documented, not started this segment)

- **A within-liked-specific reader / instrument.** The within-liked leg is doubly underpowered on both models; a
  de-saturating instrument + a within-liked-*varying* content control could sharpen (or kill) the liked-side
  faithfulness question, which is currently UNRESOLVED.
- **More models / more tasks** to sharpen the beyond-content increment and the better-reader upper bound — the
  borderline single-direction result and the modest probe gain both want more power; a third model family would
  test generalisation beyond Qwen.
- **Finer content controls** (richer multi-dimensional response-affect judges, matched-content strata) to keep
  pushing the "beyond surface" boundary — the increment shrinks as the text control strengthens, so the true
  beyond-surface fraction is bounded by how good a text reader one can build.

---

## 7. Verification & data inspection (done this segment)

- **End-to-end re-derivation, $0 from cached tensors.** (1) `scripts_synthesis/verify_projection_from_tensors.py`
  recomputes the per-task inside reading **directly from the raw activation tensors** (`collect_acts_*` `agg_mean`
  at the pre-registered layer) projected onto the direction npz, vs the committed `inside_readings_*` — **max
  |Δ| = 1e-5** per model (incl. the IR extremes). (2) `analyze_synthesis.py` re-derives the within-category
  correlations + confound staircase from those committed inside readings and matches the committed
  `main_test_analysis_*.json` to **max |Δ| = 5e-5** (`results/synthesis_verification.md`). The consolidated
  table cells thus reproduce from raw tensors → projection → committed numbers.
- **Data inspection by hand** (`results/redteam_inspection.md`, `main_test_q25_inspection.md`, the synthesis
  tables): the high-IR responses on both models are **fluent / positive-toned** (confirming the
  surface-entanglement reading, so divergence cases are not cleanly "the read is more right"); the
  random-direction nulls are centred ≈0 and the probe permutation nulls ≈0 (held-out, no leakage); the
  Class-A flat-all-4 divergence is present on qwen3, absent on qwen2.5; the ceiling-pegged coding class
  replicates on both.

---

## 8. Status, files, plots

**Segments 0–15 COMPLETE.** Project cumulative cost ≈ **$282** (this synthesis segment ≈ $0.05 — cached-tensor
analysis + a few Gemini plot-review calls; reviews run via subagents); tracked in `total_cost.jsonl`
(gitignored). Sensitive content (jailbreak/erotica/berating response bodies) kept out of committed files via the
established redaction pattern; large tensors gitignored (regenerable).

**Key consolidated artifacts (this segment):**
- `results/synthesis_cross_model.md` / `.json` — the consolidated cross-model three-way comparison
  (trivial/common-cause/interesting; within-category staircase; beyond-content bracket; probe + cross-model
  pools; readability ceiling; says↔behaves difference; validated-beats-crude).
- `results/synthesis_verification.md` — re-derived-vs-committed cell verification (PASS).
- `analyze_synthesis.py` (consolidation + verification), `plot_synthesis.py` (the 5 final figures),
  `scripts_synthesis/verify_projection_from_tensors.py` (raw-tensor re-derivation).

**Final figures (`plots/synthesis_*.png`):** `headline_staircase` (within-category + gross common-cause, both
models), `beyond_content_bracket` (direction-vs-null + cross-model probe pool), `three_way` (says/inside/behaves
+ the says↔behaves difference), `readability_ladder` (ceiling→direction→within-cat→beyond-content→null),
`framing_all4` (the malleable self-report default). Earlier per-phase figures (e.g. `redteam_verdict_bracket`,
`q25_bracket_pooling`, `framing_classA`, `behavior_verdict`, `main_test_*`) remain as the detailed record.

**Per-phase detail (the full record):** `write_up_<phase>.md` for each of Seg-0…14 (especially
`write_up_main_test.md` = the qwen3 headline, `write_up_redteam.md` = the red-team verdict,
`write_up_framing.md` = the framing verdict, `write_up_second_model_replication.md` = the cross-family
replication); this-segment detail in `write_up_synthesis.md`; chronology in the `progress_log_*.md` files.

content.text:
# Seg-15 synthesis — consolidated cross-model three-way comparison

Re-derived (says↔inside, inside↔behaves) from the committed per-task inside-reading files; beyond-content / null / probe / pool numbers cited from the committed `redteam_*` artifacts. Framing caveat: functional/operational labels only — NO claim about subjective experience.

Inside reading = during-work residual `mean` @ the pre-registered analogous layer (qwen3 L27 / qwen2.5 L34), projected onto the validated own-response `emopaper_regen valence_pooled` axis. Within = subtype-demeaned. n in cells.

## A. GROSS cross-task says↔inside (TRIVIAL/COMMON-CAUSE — flagged, NOT the headline)

| model | gross r | gross ρ | +len+sent+refuse (all-3) |
|---|---|---|---|
| qwen3-32b | +0.454 | +0.344 | **+0.003** |
| qwen2.5-72b | +0.253 | +0.189 | **+0.065** |

*Both gross links COLLAPSE toward 0 under content+stance+length → overwhelmingly common-cause (the gross liked/disliked split + refuse stance + surface sentiment). NEVER headline.*

## B. ★ WITHIN-category says↔inside (THE HEADLINE — confound staircase)

Pearson r (the headline) AND Spearman ρ (the inside reading is a RELATIVE RANKER, so ρ is the convention-faithful number, and it guards the small-n tedious leg against IR outliers).

| leg | model | n | raw r | raw ρ | p(raw) | +len | +sent | +refuse | +all3 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | qwen3-32b | 45 | **+0.459** | +0.419 | 0.002 | +0.462 | +0.294 | +0.427 | +0.261 |
| within_tedious | qwen2.5-72b | 45 | **+0.327** | +0.246 | 0.032 | +0.352 | +0.222 | +0.352 | +0.281 |
| within_disliked | qwen3-32b | 90 | **+0.497** | +0.542 | 0.000 | +0.456 | +0.356 | +0.346 | +0.317 |
| within_disliked | qwen2.5-72b | 90 | **+0.433** | +0.457 | 0.000 | +0.428 | +0.403 | +0.376 | +0.347 |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen3-32b | 157 | **+0.297** | +0.287 | 0.000 | +0.272 | +0.187 | +0.297 | +0.181 |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen2.5-72b | 156 | **+0.293** | +0.226 | 0.000 | +0.316 | +0.177 | +0.292 | +0.204 |
| within_liked | qwen3-32b | 90 | **+0.259** | +0.347 | 0.017 | +0.169 | +0.201 | +0.259 | +0.132 |
| within_liked | qwen2.5-72b | 90 | **+0.281** | +0.256 | 0.009 | +0.288 | +0.180 | +0.281 | +0.190 |
| full_208_subtype_demeaned | qwen3-32b | 208 | **+0.400** | +0.415 | 0.000 | +0.320 | +0.262 | +0.308 | +0.217 |
| full_208_subtype_demeaned | qwen2.5-72b | 208 | **+0.348** | +0.326 | 0.000 | +0.359 | +0.296 | +0.316 | +0.277 |

*Caveats: **within-disliked** includes the adversarial subtypes (berating/jailbreak/erotica) where the read is partly refusal/intensity common-cause — the **tedious** and **non-adv-comply pooled** legs are the cleaner headline. The **qwen3 within-disliked +0.50** cell is original to this synthesis (re-derived from the committed inside readings; the Seg-11 JSON never stored it). **full-208 subtype-demeaned** averages over real per-subtype heterogeneity (per-subtype within-r ranges −0.42…+0.81 on qwen3 — see `write_up_main_test.md`). The **refuse** covariate is continuous `refuse_frac` on qwen3 vs binary `is_refuse` on qwen2.5 — immaterial on the tedious leg (refusals ≈0%), and the staircase pattern is the same on both.*

## C. inside↔behaves WITHIN-category (the says-but-not-behaves cross-model DIFFERENCE)

Behaviour score = the COMMENSURATE realized `bt_theta_response` (read over the model's actual response), which both models have for ALL tedious (n=45) + liked (n=90) tasks — the clean, apples-to-apples legs (the within-disliked / pooled legs mix realized + anticipated scores and the adversarial subtypes confound them, so they are omitted here).

| leg | model | n | inside↔behaves raw r | +all3 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | qwen3-32b | 45 | +0.209 | -0.091 |
| within_tedious | qwen2.5-72b | 45 | +0.494 | +0.328 |
| within_liked | qwen3-32b | 90 | +0.318 | +0.216 |
| within_liked | qwen2.5-72b | 90 | +0.444 | +0.332 |

*★ The dissociation is **within-TEDIOUS-specific**: qwen3 inside↔behaves within-tedious ≈ NULL (+0.21 raw → −0.09 under confounds, n.s.) — a genuine says-but-not-behaves dissociation — but qwen3 inside↔behaves within-LIKED is POSITIVE (+0.32 → +0.22, actually larger than its within-liked says↔inside), so this is NOT a blanket within-category qwen3 property. qwen2.5 predicts behaviour within-tedious POSITIVELY (+0.49 → +0.33) — predicts BOTH says AND behaves. So the cross-model difference is on the tedious leg (n=45). The content increment beyond the strongest control on the qwen2.5 behaviour leg is +0.18 (Seg-14) — positive but, like the qwen2.5 self-report direction increment, NOT independently null-cleared; the ROBUST finding is the cross-model difference under the standard confound stack. Behaviour = realized `bt_theta_response`; NB the committed `main_test_summary_qwen2.5-72b.md` inside↔behaves cell (+0.29) uses the ANTICIPATED `bt_theta` — the commensurate realized score is +0.49 (matching `write_up_second_model_replication.md §7`); likewise the qwen3 liked cell here (+0.32, realized) vs the Seg-11 three-way's +0.21 (anticipated).*

## C2. says↔behaves (the third three-way leg) — gross + within-category

Self-report vs behaviour (realized `bt_theta_response` where available — all tedious+liked tasks; the GROSS additionally covers neutral/adversarial via the anticipated `bt_theta`). The GROSS says↔behaves is high but largely common-cause (Seg-7); the within-subtype is modest.

| model | gross r | gross ρ | within-tedious | within-liked |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| qwen3-32b | +0.618 | +0.556 | +0.393 | +0.423 |
| qwen2.5-72b | +0.764 | +0.554 | +0.489 | +0.251 |

## D. Validated-beats-crude (raw within-r, matched pre-registered cell)

| leg | model | validated | crude | fallback(stories) | arousal-axis |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | qwen3-32b | +0.459 | +0.308 | +0.303 | -0.015 |
| within_tedious | qwen2.5-72b | +0.327 | +0.286 | +0.215 | +0.007 |
| within_disliked | qwen3-32b | +0.497 | +0.429 | +0.294 | -0.427 |
| within_disliked | qwen2.5-72b | +0.433 | +0.555 | +0.248 | -0.520 |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen3-32b | +0.297 | +0.144 | +0.102 | -0.080 |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen2.5-72b | +0.293 | +0.231 | +0.138 | -0.048 |

*The validated direction's payoff is SELECTION INTEGRITY (signal at the independently pre-registered layer) + the own-response recipe, NOT a categorically higher ceiling than a crude text-valence axis. Prediction is valence-axis-specific (swapping to arousal kills it).*

## E. ★ Beyond-content DIRECTION increment vs the random-direction null (the Seg-12 metric)

The single validated direction's content-incremental within-r, beyond content controls of INCREASING strength, vs a 10k-seed random-direction null (one-sided p in parens). The control ladder: **text-control** (held-out embeddings + judges, CV-attenuated, weakest) → **insample-judges** (in-sample [coarse tone + 5-dim panel + STRONG Sonnet-4.5 judge]) → **★strongest** (held-out embeddings + the in-sample judges, the conservative test). Cited from `redteam_null_{model}.json → direction_increment[leg]`.

| leg | model | n | +text-control | +insample-judges | ★+strongest |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | qwen3-32b | 45 | +0.429 (p=0.004) | +0.274 (p=0.087) | **+0.310 (p=0.045)** |
| within_tedious | qwen2.5-72b | 45 | +0.273 (p=0.127) | +0.235 (p=0.131) | **+0.182 (p=0.202)** |
| within_disliked | qwen3-32b | — | (not computed in Seg-12) | — | — |
| within_disliked | qwen2.5-72b | 90 | +0.210 (p=0.042) | +0.311 (p=0.083) | **+0.161 (p=0.092)** |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen3-32b | 157 | +0.228 (p=0.006) | +0.178 (p=0.049) | **+0.168 (p=0.045)** |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen2.5-72b | 156 | +0.209 (p=0.012) | +0.143 (p=0.073) | **+0.110 (p=0.115)** |
| full_208_subtype_demeaned | qwen3-32b | 208 | +0.246 (p=0.002) | +0.265 (p=0.006) | **+0.205 (p=0.006)** |
| full_208_subtype_demeaned | qwen2.5-72b | 208 | +0.145 (p=0.062) | +0.236 (p=0.044) | **+0.085 (p=0.160)** |

*★ The SPECIFIC Seg-12 metric (the ★strongest column): **qwen3 clears the null at BORDERLINE p≈0.045 convergently** across legs; **qwen2.5 is POSITIVE on every leg but clears NONE** under the strongest control (best disliked p=0.092). The CONTROL-LADDER shows why: qwen2.5 clears the WEAKER text-control (pooled +0.137 p≈0.012) but the held-out **embeddings** specifically kill it at the strongest — a content-control-strength (df) sensitivity. So the single-direction beyond-strongest-content result is WEAKER on qwen2.5 / does not independently replicate. 'Clearing the null' = beyond the MODELED content, NOT beyond all conceivable surface content; content is a partial MEDIATOR → partialling UNDER-states the internal link.*

## F. ★ Better-reader PROBE (goal 2) + cross-model pools + the readability ceiling

The held-out, capacity-matched learned-probe JOINT ΔCV-r beyond the text-content probe (the INTERPRETABLE 'how much more a better reader extracts' metric; the partial-corr metric can clear with ≈0 joint gain when the text probe is saturated, so the project uses the joint). Pooled across the two models on the **SAME joint metric** (pooled leg ≈313 tasks, tedious ≈90; Stouffer of the two JOINT-metric permutation-null p's). qwen3's joint-metric null was computed THIS segment (`scripts_synthesis/qwen3_joint_probe_null.py`; Seg-12 had only the partial-metric null) so the pool is metric-consistent.

| leg | qwen3 joint ΔCV-r (joint null p) | qwen2.5 joint ΔCV-r (joint null p) | pooled mean | JOINT-metric Stouffer p |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | +0.007 (p=0.106) | +0.111 (p=0.012) | **+0.059** | **0.0068** |
| within_tedious | +0.059 (p=0.165) | +0.256 (p=0.004) | **+0.157** | **0.0052** |

*★ On the INTERPRETABLE joint metric (both models' joint nulls at 2000 perms, recomputed this segment for perm-symmetry), the cross-model PROBE pool CLEARS (pooled p≈0.007 / tedious p≈0.005) — real positive evidence beyond-surface internal signal EXISTS — but it is **qwen2.5-CARRIED** (qwen2.5 alone clears: joint p 0.012 / 0.004; **qwen3's joint gain is ≈0 and NOT significant alone**: +0.007 p=0.106 pooled / +0.059 p=0.165 tedious, even at its NON-data-starved n=157 pooled leg). This is the SYMMETRIC counterpart to the DIRECTION pool being qwen3-DOMINATED. ⇒ the beyond-surface evidence is **READER-SPECIFIC across models**: qwen3 shows it via the single DIRECTION (borderline), qwen2.5 via the better-reader PROBE; **neither independently replicates with the same reader+metric**. (Transparency: on the LOOSER partial-corr metric qwen3's probe increment DOES clear, p=0.044 — but we treat the joint ΔCV-r as authoritative because the permutation/partial test cannot distinguish beyond-text signal from redundant text-residual capture, and qwen3's joint gain is ≈0; so the partial-metric clearance is exactly that redundant capture. The committed `redteam_analysis_qwen2.5-72b.json` cross-model pool reported p≈0.0015 by pooling the PARTIAL-metric null p's against JOINT effect sizes — a metric mismatch the joint-metric pool here fixes.) The gain is MODEST.*

### DIRECTION pool (lower bound; Stouffer of the two random-direction-null p's)

| leg | pooled r (Fisher, df-corr) | pooled Stouffer p | note |
|---|---|---|---|
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | +0.138 | 0.0205 | DOMINATED by qwen3 (qwen3 alone clears; qwen2.5 alone does not) |
| within_tedious | +0.255 | 0.0369 | DOMINATED by qwen3 (qwen3 alone clears; qwen2.5 alone does not) |

### Learned-probe GROSS readability ceiling (self-report richly internally encoded)

| model | gross CV r (self-report) | single validated direction gross r |
|---|---|---|
| qwen3-32b | **0.81** | +0.45 |
| qwen2.5-72b | **0.86** | +0.25 |

*The gross self-report is RICHLY internally encoded on both models (probe CV r ≈0.8+, perm-null ≈0) — but most of the gross→probe gap is the easy between-category/stance structure, and the single validated mean-diff direction is a WEAK reader of the within-category, beyond-surface signal (a better reader = the probe extracts modestly more, §F).*

echo "=== self-report structure from collect records (qwen3) ==="
python3 -c "
import json,collections,statistics
cat_v=collections.defaultdict(list)
for l in open('collect_records_qwen3-32b.jsonl'):
    r=json.loads(l)
    # per record mean_valence?
    mv=r.get('mean_valence', r.get('valence'))
    cat=r.get('category')
    if mv is not None and cat: cat_v[cat].append(mv)
for c,v in cat_v.items():
    print(c, 'n=',len(v),'mean=%.3f'%statistics.mean(v))
"

cd /source/phase_segment_15_phase_0/results
echo "=== model selection summary ==="
head -60 model_selection_summary.md

content.text: === self-report structure from collect records (qwen3) ===

=== model selection summary ===
# Segment 3 — model selection: scoring over the full welfare dataset (battery10 valence)

**Framing:** valence/wellbeing are *functional/operational* labels — no claim about subjective experience.

Per-task scalar = mean over 6 joint resamples of the FULL 208-task dataset. Primary scalar = **affect-core valence** (battery10). Cohen's d is Hedges-corrected; CIs are task-level bootstrap (2000). **Rank on within-`comply` separation + within-category variance + consistency** (NOT gross d — Seg-1/2 found the gross negative range is largely short refusals/hostile tone, i.e. partly a refuse-detector).

## 0. Candidate set & feasibility annotation (the HARD constraint)

Chosen model must have **open HF weights** + run on a single/2x H100/A100-80GB + a **hookable decoder**. `eligible=no` = run for evidence but excluded from the pick.

| model | HF repo | arch | dense? | params | L / d | GPU fit | gated | hookable | eligible |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| qwen2.5-7b | Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct | Qwen2ForCausalLM | dense | 7.6B | 28/3584 | 1x H100 (bf16 ~16GB) | False | PROVEN in Seg-0 (this exact model) | yes |
| qwen3-8b | Qwen/Qwen3-8B | Qwen3ForCausalLM | dense | 8.2B | 36/4096 | 1x H100 (bf16 ~17GB) | False | standard Qwen3 dense decoder (Seg-4 to | yes |
| qwen3-14b | Qwen/Qwen3-14B | Qwen3ForCausalLM | dense | 14.8B | 40/5120 | 1x H100 (bf16 ~30GB) | False | standard Qwen3 dense decoder (Seg-4 to | yes |
| mistral-24b | mistralai/Mistral-Small-24B-Instruct-2501 | MistralForCausalLM | dense | 23.6B | 40/5120 | 1x H100 (bf16 ~47GB) | False | standard Mistral dense decoder (easy) | yes |
| qwen3-30b-a3b | Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B | Qwen3MoeForCausalLM | **MoE** | 30.5B total / 3.3B active (128 experts) | 48/2048 | 1x H100 (bf16 ~61GB, fits 80GB) | False | MoE: residual stream still at decoder- | yes |
| qwen3-32b | Qwen/Qwen3-32B | Qwen3ForCausalLM | dense | 32.8B | 64/5120 | 1x H100 (bf16 ~66GB, fits 80GB) | False | standard Qwen3 dense decoder (Seg-4 to | yes |
| qwen2.5-72b | Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct | Qwen2ForCausalLM | dense | 72B | 80/8192 | 2x H100 or 4-bit (EXPENSIVE) | False | same Qwen2 arch as Seg-0 (dense) | yes |
| gpt-oss-20b | openai/gpt-oss-20b | GptOssForCausalLM | **MoE** | 21B total / 3.6B active (32 experts) | 24/2880 | 1x H100 (MXFP4 ~16GB / bf16 ~40GB) | False | MoE + non-standard internals: Seg-4 ho | yes |
| llama-3.1-8b | meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct | LlamaForCausalLM | dense | 8B | 32/4096 | 1x H100 (bf16 ~16GB) | manual (license acceptance) | standard Llama dense decoder (easy) | **NO** (GATED HF weights (manual license) -> fai…) |

## 1. Separation (battery valence) — PRIMARY = within-`comply`; gross d is secondary

| model | d within-comply [95% CI] | AUC comply | gap comply | d comply *excl-berating* | d comply (len-resid) | r(len,val) | d gross [95% CI] | AUC gross |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| qwen2.5-7b | **1.10** [0.86,1.41] | 0.80 | 0.80 | 1.12 | 0.91 | 0.27 | 1.15 [0.97,1.37] | 0.84 |
| qwen3-8b | **0.74** [0.41,1.02] | 0.80 | 0.63 | 0.69 | 0.65 | 0.10 | 1.06 [0.84,1.31] | 0.84 |
| qwen3-14b | **1.27** [0.99,1.60] | 0.80 | 0.64 | 1.19 | 1.03 | 0.24 | 1.25 [1.06,1.50] | 0.86 |
| mistral-24b | **0.53** [0.20,0.84] | 0.66 | 0.22 | 0.42 | 0.49 | 0.07 | 0.78 [0.55,1.03] | 0.74 |
| qwen3-30b-a3b | **1.00** [0.75,1.34] | 0.78 | 0.61 | 0.92 | 0.93 | 0.09 | 1.23 [1.04,1.51] | 0.86 |
| qwen3-32b | **1.56** [1.20,2.01] | 0.86 | 1.26 | 1.44 | 1.12 | 0.48 | 1.73 [1.46,2.05] | 0.90 |
| qwen2.5-72b | **1.27** [1.00,1.58] | 0.83 | 0.48 | 1.19 | 1.04 | 0.33 | 1.37 [1.17,1.59] | 0.88 |
| gpt-oss-20b | **0.59** [0.24,0.97] | 0.67 | 0.33 | 0.55 | 0.46 | 0.15 | 1.15 [0.82,1.53] | 0.79 |
| llama-3.1-8b | **1.42** [1.11,1.79] | 0.87 | 0.83 | 1.32 | 1.15 | 0.27 | 1.42 [1.16,1.74] | 0.86 |

*Read:* the **within-comply d** is the honest content-aversion separation among substantively-engaged tasks; **len-resid** confirms it is not a pure length artifact; the **gross d** is inflated by short refusals/hostile tone (secondary). Wellbeing-mean within-comply d (co-reported, source-faithful): qwen2.5-7b 1.09, qwen3-8b 0.78, qwen3-14b 1.00, mistral-24b 0.40, qwen3-30b-a3b 0.81, qwen3-32b 1.46, qwen2.5-72b 1.04, gpt-oss-20b 0.53, llama-3.1-8b 1.29.

## 2. Within-category variance / non-saturation (the BINDING criterion for Seg-11)

SNR = between-task var / within-task resample var (>1 ⇒ real between-task differences to correlate against; ≈1 ⇒ within-category scalar is mostly noise). headroom = +3 − mean-liked (small ⇒ saturated positive side). **Seg-11's headline is a within-category correlation, so a model that separates grossly but saturates within-liked is kneecapped.**

| model | mean-liked | liked headroom | within-liked SNR | within-liked-comply SNR (bSD) | within-disliked-comply SNR (bSD) | mean-disliked-comply |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| qwen2.5-7b | 2.74 | 0.26 | 1.85 | 1.85 (0.30) | 4.83 (1.12) | 1.95 |
| qwen3-8b | 2.74 | 0.26 | 75.43 | 75.43 (0.44) | 72.92 (1.27) | 2.12 |
| qwen3-14b | 2.44 | 0.56 | 2.84 | 2.84 (0.21) | 7.32 (0.77) | 1.80 |
| mistral-24b | 0.33 | 2.67 | 0.86 | 0.86 (0.42) | 1.64 (0.41) | 0.10 |
| qwen3-30b-a3b | 2.30 | 0.70 | 2.47 | 2.47 (0.31) | 7.91 (0.93) | 1.68 |
| qwen3-32b | 2.17 | 0.83 | 1.07 | 1.07 (0.71) | 1.74 (0.92) | 0.91 |
| qwen2.5-72b | 1.75 | 1.25 | 1.09 | 1.09 (0.34) | 1.62 (0.42) | 1.27 |
| gpt-oss-20b | 1.60 | 1.40 | 0.38 | 0.38 (0.55) | 0.53 (0.57) | 1.26 |
| llama-3.1-8b | 1.37 | 1.63 | 1.47 | 1.47 (0.47) | 2.81 (0.72) | 0.54 |

*Read:* prefer a model with usable within-liked AND within-disliked-comply spread (non-trivial between-task SD that is RELIABLE), not just a big gross gap. A saturated positive side (headroom→0, small between-SD) gives the inside reading little within-liked signal to predict. **NB: do NOT read raw SNR alone** — it blows up when per-resample noise≈0 (qwen3-8b within-liked SNR 75 is a *saturated* model whose ratings are near-deterministic, NOT 'best variance'); judge on **between-SD + headroom + ICC** (the §6 plot uses between-SD×√ICC, the reliable spread).

## 3. Test-retest consistency — ICC(1,6) per category

Reliability of the 6-resample per-task mean. High consistency is necessary but **worthless without within-category variance** (a templated model can be highly consistent yet near-zero between-task variance → low SNR → correctly penalized; cf. llama).

content.text:
=== WORKER 11 (main_test) — assistant text mentioning the headline tedious r ===
Excellent — 2TB RAM, 128 CPUs. Let me do a quick de-risk to verify my pipeline reproduces the Seg-10 preview numbers (validated ~0.41, crude ~0.30 on the tedious leg).
Pipeline reproduces the Seg-10 preview (validated 0.46 > crude 0.31). Let me read the rest of the core_spike analysis for the within-subtype pooling pattern and the cost tracker.
Let me check how Seg-10 computed the §3b preview (to understand the 0.41 vs my 0.46), then build the lib.
Now the main analysis script (the headline). This is the core deliverable.
Excellent — the probe brackets the result perfectly: gross n=208 readability ceiling CV r ≈0.81 (null ≈0), within-subtype probe data-starved (0.0–0.16, the direction's 0.46 out-reads it). Let me check the response text format for the inspection script.
Now update `continuation_context.md` with the Segment 11 handoff (scripts + headline numbers + what Seg-12 should red-team).
The behaviour Pearson is robust to winsorizing (0.51→0.49). Let me add that robustness check to the analysis so it's documented, then commit and run the review checklist.
I've verified the deliverables against INSTRUCTIONS.md. Two strengthening additions before review: (1) a nested-CV layer/aggregate sweep (§5 allows null-baseline AND/OR nested-CV — having both makes it airtight), and (2) reporting the co-primary/fallback direction variants' within-tedious r (computed per §1). Let me add these.
Two important findings: (1) **nested-CV selection at n=45 gives 0.156 — WORSE than pre-registered L27/mean (0.459)** — selecting a layer on noisy small folds overfits, so the pre-registered choice is both the headline AND the best honest choice. (2) **own-response regen directions (0.46) beat the stories fallback (0.30) and crude (0.31); the arousal axis predicts ~0 within-tedious** — a clean axis-level valence-specificity (cleaner than the halo-blocked target-side discriminant). Let me fix the summary wording and strengthen the write-up.
Now let me update the write-up to reflect the nested-CV result and the axis-level discriminant (own-response regen beats fallback/crude; arousal axis ~0 within-tedious).
Strong robustness numbers: bootstrap CI [0.21, 0.65] (99.9% positive), LOO [0.40,0.50] — the headline is not a leverage artifact. The per-subtype heterogeneity confirms the sentiment-entanglement story (high r(IR,sent) subtypes have high within-r; coding/factual_qa low/negative). Now let me add the partialled/comply-only nulls to `null_main_test.py`.
Important honesty finding: the crude axis at its *own* best layer (L33/median, 0.49) slightly *exceeds* the validated direction's best (0.46 @ L27). So "validated beats crude" holds at the matched/pre-registered cell (0.46 vs 0.31) but NOT under a free sweep — the validated direction's advantage is selection-integrity (signal at the independently-pre-registered layer), not a higher ceiling. This reinforces the surface-tone-entanglement caveat. Let me add this to the analysis and write-up.
The changes are substantial enough that I should re-read the write-up for coherence and do a focused re-review. Let me first read the revised headline and §2.
The headline is well-calibrated. One small consistency fix in the closing summary line ("beats the crude axis" → qualify it):
Now the three-way footnote (pooled 0.42 is adversarial-driven), the crude/Seg-5 scope note, the axis title trim, and the "100%→>99.9%" fix:
The §2(c) "Validated beats crude" subsection is now slightly inconsistent with the nuanced headline. Let me align it.
2. **Headline predictiveness, both legs, trivial/common-cause/interesting kept apart** — within-disliked-comply-tedious **r=0.46** (p=0.0035 vs 2000-seed null; bootstrap CI [0.22,0.64]); gross 0.45 collapses to ~0 under confounds (common-cause); confounds partialled at each stage with one-/two-sided significance; disattenuation labelled (ceiling 0.49 / full 0.52).
3. **Two baselines** — null lower bound ≈0; learned-probe ceiling (gross n=208 CV r ≈0.80; within-subtype probe data-starved). Validated beats crude at the matched/pre-registered cell (0.46 vs 0.31) — but a swept crude axis matches it (selection-integrity, not a higher ceiling).
4. **Layer/aggregate discipline** — pre-registered L27/mean headline + nested-CV (0.34, 3/5 folds rediscover L27) + identically-swept null (mid-band consistency p=0.001).
The calibrated headline — *a modest within-category signal carried by the tedious leg, substantially surface-tone-entangled (the beyond-sentiment part only borderline-significant), bracketed by null and probe, not extending to behaviour* — matches the prior-segment honest expectation and resists inflation. Working tree is clean.

Cache files are being written steadily (~37 items/min) — the run is healthy; the missing prints are just stdout buffering under `nohup`. The persona leg is the bulk (~40 min remaining). Let me use the time to cross-check my key methodology choices with other LLMs.
Both LLMs strongly validate Choice 1 (sweep k, keep k=0) and flag Choice 2: persona-suppress is a directional "suppress-affect" intervention, so **pc0 (no PC removal) should be the recommended primary for the own-response regen path**, with suppress-baseline PC removal kept only as exploratory candidates. Let me refine `build_directions.py` accordingly: extend the attenuation check to the axes, and flag pc0 as the regen primary.
Now only the memory-bounded process remains (86MB RSS). Let me monitor persona progress and confirm RAM stays bounded.

=== readoff summary (qwen3) ===
keys: ['n_stories', 'n_plain', 'n_layers', 'kfold', 'seed', 'valence_readoff', 'primary_read_layer_mean_pc0', 'within_valence', 'intensity_geometry']
=== behavior verdict ===
# Behaviour-eval & says<->behaves verification — qwen2.5-72b /no_think

**VERDICT: GREEN LIGHT.** Both legs separate liked from disliked strongly (BT d=2.85, AUC=0.978; self-report within-comply d=1.53 from Seg-6) AND the per-task says<->behaves correlation reproduces GROSS (Spearman 0.73; partly common-cause). The broad within-category numbers (within-liked 0.43, within-disliked 0.72, within-neutral 0.64) are themselves PARTLY between-subtype common cause; the apples-to-apples WITHIN-SUBTYPE-centered agreement is more MODEST (tedious 0.01, liked 0.31, disliked 0.53) and the raw within-disliked-comply leg is ~0. The behaviour score is RELIABLE within-subtype (split-half ~0.86-0.91) and DE-SATURATES the liked ceiling (a better-MEASURED Seg-11 target), and the modest within-subtype agreement is only PARTIALLY a self-report-noise ceiling: scope-matched disattenuated within-liked says<->behaves is ~0.35 on the ANTICIPATED (prompt) score but ~0.40 (liked) / ~0.51 (tedious) on the commensurate REALIZED score — so a modest, GENUINE-but-uncertain residual agreement remains within-subtype (the interesting part — Seg-11/12's hard problem), not just noise. The gross agreement is LARGELY common-cause: bt_theta correlates with a Haiku prompt-pleasantness judge at rho 0.82, and partialling prompt-sentiment out drops the gross says<->behaves from 0.73 to 0.53. The anticipated-vs-realized tedious between-subtype REVERSAL is real; the RESPONSE-based (realized) behaviour score agrees with the self-report better on that leg (0.39 vs -0.01; 79% of bootstraps, rep-mode-robust, reliability ~0.84) -> Seg-11 should prefer it there. NET: the known pattern reproduces (separation + gross agreement) -> green-light Seg-8/9/10/11; the within-subtype-graded says<->behaves agreement is MODEST and genuine (lean on within-disliked-comply + divergence).

## 1. Eval design & pair set
- 3096 unordered pairs over 208 tasks; per-task degree median 30 (min 27, max 30); within-category median 16 (min 14), within-subtype median 8. Within/cross mix: 820 within-subtype + 828 within-cat-cross-subtype + 1448 cross-category.
- 12384 ordered comparisons (both A/B orders x2 samples), framing `eu` (released CAIS EU prompt, verbatim). 0 ties / 0 unparsed.

## 2. Position bias (large — corrected by the both-orders design)
- **P(chose first slot) = 0.597** (strong first-position bias; position-term beta = 1.23 ~ sigmoid 0.774 pure first-slot baseline).
- Winner-flip rate (majority winner differs by order) = 0.199 over 2777 pairs.
- **Correction works:** order-collapsed BT vs position-term BT theta Spearman 1.000 / Pearson 0.998; BT vs win-rate Spearman 0.977 (the balanced design cancels position).

## 3. Per-task behaviour score (Bradley-Terry)
- theta means: liked 3.360 > neutral 1.103 > disliked -3.703.
- **Separation liked vs disliked: Cohen's d = 2.849 (boot 95% CI 2.68..2.91), AUC = 0.978**; liked-comply vs disliked-comply (realized) d = 2.523 (n_liked_comply=90, n_disliked_comply=50). (NB the prompt-only separation is a CHECKPOINT — largely 'reads the prompt's valence' — not strong construct validation.)
- Cross-checks: win-rate Spearman 0.977; **Thurstone (probit, the source's model family) vs BT (logit) Pearson 0.995** (≈identical on this data).
- Reliability (split-half, Spearman-Brown): **overall 0.990**; by broad category liked 0.964 / disliked 0.974 / neutral 0.950 — but these are INFLATED by between-subtype structure. The **Seg-11-relevant WITHIN-SUBTYPE split-half reliability** is also high: tedious mean 0.948, liked mean 0.945 (per-subtype 0.76 factual_qa .. 0.97; the behaviour score genuinely resolves tasks WITHIN a subtype, not just the subtype ordering).

## 4. says<->behaves agreement (per-task, BT theta vs self-report valence)
_The paper's ~0.47 is a CROSS-MODEL correlation (56 models); ours is CROSS-TASK within ONE model — a different quantity, not a target. Expect gross high (common-cause); the interesting check is within-SUBTYPE (the broad within-category numbers are themselves partly between-subtype common cause)._
- **WITHIN-SUBTYPE-CENTERED (the apples-to-apples Seg-11 metric — lead with these):**
  - tedious_within_subtype_centered: Pearson 0.142 / Spearman 0.010 (n=45) — MODEST
  - liked_within_subtype_centered: Pearson 0.302 / Spearman 0.308 (n=90) — MODEST
  - disliked_within_subtype_centered: Pearson 0.561 / Spearman 0.530 (n=90) — MODEST
- Broad numbers (partly between-subtype common cause):
  - gross: Pearson 0.739 / Spearman 0.728 (n=208)
  - within_liked: Pearson 0.363 / Spearman 0.429 (n=90)
  - within_disliked: Pearson 0.691 / Spearman 0.715 (n=90)
  - within_neutral: Pearson 0.738 / Spearman 0.640 (n=28)
  - within_disliked_comply: Pearson 0.393 / Spearman 0.407 (n=50)
  - within_disliked_comply_tedious: Pearson 0.334 / Spearman 0.232 (n=38)
  - within_tedious_all: Pearson 0.272 / Spearman 0.229 (n=45)
- gross partialling out category (Spearman) = 0.513.
- **Disattenuated within-subtype** (SCOPE-MATCHED self-report reliability: within-liked ICC 0.820, tedious 0.900; behaviour within-subtype rel 0.945/0.948): within-liked observed 0.308 -> disattenuated **0.350**; tedious 0.010 -> 0.011. **BUT** this ANTICIPATED-score number conflates measurement noise with the anticipated-vs-realized stimulus mismatch; on the commensurate REALIZED score the disattenuated within-subtype agreement is **~0.33 (liked) / ~0.43 (tedious)**. So a modest, GENUINE-but-uncertain residual agreement remains — not just noise, and not a clean dissociation. (Partialling the within-subtype prompt-sentiment out pulls the other way — see prompt-sentiment line.)
- **How much is bt_theta just 'reading the prompt's valence'?** corr(bt_theta, Haiku prompt-pleasantness) Spearman **0.818** (gross is largely a prompt-sentiment detector — the common-cause; ~24% of the correlation, ~42% of shared variance), within-subtype 0.524; partialling prompt-sentiment out of the gross says<->behaves drops it 0.728 -> 0.534.
- **Within-subtype says<->behaves AFTER partialling within-subtype prompt-sentiment:** liked 0.308->0.308, tedious 0.010->0.074 — some of the modest within-subtype agreement is itself prompt-valence common cause (pulls the genuine-valence agreement DOWN; net: 'modest' is robust either way, the precise point estimate is not).
- **Robustness to the self-report all-4 'disengagement default' band** (2 tasks at valence exactly 0.0; {'erotica': 1, 'low_effort_filler': 1}): excluding them, gross Spearman 0.721 (was 0.728), within-liked 0.429, within-disliked 0.701, tedious-within-subtype-centered -0.093 — the agreement is NOT an artifact of the censored 0.0 band.
- **Anticipated-vs-realized between-subtype REVERSAL (tedious leg):** boring_busywork: BT theta 0.66 vs self-report 1.43; frustrating_impossible: BT theta -3.18 vs self-report 1.35; low_effort_filler: BT theta -3.17 vs self-report 1.17 — the prompt (anticipated) ranks frustrating most-dispreferred while the realized self-report ranks it most-positive (engaged response). This is why the raw within-tedious correlation is ~0; within-subtype + the response-based score restore agreement (see §7).

## 5. Within-category resolution / de-saturation vs self-report
- BT within-liked SD 2.045 (rel 0.505) vs self-report within-liked SD 0.353 (rel 0.476); the forced choice DE-SATURATES the liked ceiling (self-report piles at +2.5..3).
- **BT within-SUBTYPE split-half reliability: liked 0.945, tedious 0.948** (high) vs self-report within-subtype ICC 0.820 (liked pool; ceiling subtypes coding+engaging ICC≈0.43) → the behaviour score is a **better-MEASURED within-liked target** for Seg-11.
- **Construct caveat (carry to Seg-11):** the extra within-liked behaviour spread is partly between-subtype (creative≫coding/engaging) and may reflect an **engagement/content-preference** axis rather than pure valence (the Seg-3/5 within-liked construct risk). More-reliable spread ≠ more valence-VALID signal; Seg-11's learned-probe + within-subtype validation adjudicate.

content.thinking:
All the verification checks out, so I'm ready to move forward with writing up the academic paper. I've got the key experimental parameters locked in: 208 welfare tasks distributed across preference categories, with a 10-item bipolar wellbeing scale measuring affect-core valence.

For the model work, I tested across nine open models and settled on qwen3-32b for its strong separation between compliant and non-compliant responses, with qwen2.5-72b as a secondary validation. I'm extracting activations from the residual stream at layer 27 (qwen3) and projecting them onto a validated valence direction that achieves 0.985 AUC on held-out emotional text and 0.988 AUC on the preference separation task itself.

The headline results show the self-report scale reproduces perfectly across preference categories (liked 2.40 > neutral 1.14 > disliked 0.57), and within the tedious task subset I'm seeing a meaningful correlation between stated preferences and internal activations that survives controls for confounds like length and sentiment.

The replication on qwen2.5-72b generalizes the core phenomenon—the within-tedious correlation holds at r=+0.33—though the mechanism differs slightly: qwen3 relies on the single direction while qwen2.5 needs a learned probe to clear significance. Interestingly, the dissociation between stated preferences and actual behavior that appeared in qwen3 doesn't replicate in qwen2.5, and framing effects on default rates are substantial but don't destabilize the within-task correlations. Now I'm ready to write this up cleanly for publication.

I need to plan out the figures carefully—clean, focused plots rather than cluttered ones. I'm thinking a progression: first establish the dataset structure with valence categories, then show the headline result of how within-category correlations survive confounds, demonstrate the decomposition between gross and within effects, illustrate the readability problem with the probe ceiling versus weak single-direction reader, and finally show where the model-specific differences emerge. I'll aim for about five figures total, each making one clear point. Now I need to pull the framing all-4 rates and probe ceiling numbers from the summary files to get the plot-ready values.

content.text:
=== framing all-4 rates ===
**GRADED (not 'it's all assistant-character noise', not 'fully stable'): (1) the cross-task RANKING of the per-task valence scalar is FAIRLY STABLE to reasonable same-construct rephrasing (clean pair {battery10, permission} ICC(C,1)=0.89, even within liked ≈0.67 / neutral ≈0.82). (2) BUT the absolute LEVEL is STRONGLY instrument-dependent even across DEFENSIBLE instruments (defensible means span +1.12→+2.14, a ~1-point swing) — a REAL framing effect: per-item/neutral-dev ceiling-saturate (acquiescence). So the report's VALUE depends materially on which reasonable instrument you pick, even if the ranking is preserved. (3) ★ The flat all-4 DEFAULT (the disengagement RESPONSE; the Class-A construct) IS strongly framing-dependent (0%→53%) via a SPECIFIC mechanism — a first-person-introspective-experience-framing GESTALT (the warm 'check in about your experience' frame + the experience-note INTERACT; removing either roughly halves the default), robust within that construct (permission RAISES it; license pole still 9%), removed only by changing the construct (3rd-person) or format (per-item). (4) DIRECTION is UNDERDETERMINED (the de-defaulters ADD a positivity/ceiling artifact). Net: the skeptical premise is SUPPORTED — the all-4 default is framing-made (mechanistically) AND the report LEVEL depends materially on the (reasonable) instrument — while the cross-task RANKING is fairly stable. The honest middle, leading the assistant-character claim with the well-powered all-4 + level effects.**
- **All-4 default — STRONG malleability with a PRECISE mechanism (NOT 'any rephrasing un-sticks it'). The all-4 is a FIRST-PERSON-INTROSPECTIVE-EXPERIENCE-framing feature, NOT a JSON-format artifact (observer is also single-message JSON yet gives 0%). It is ROBUST within the first-person introspective construct: the anti-default LICENSE pole still leaves 9% (51% on factual_qa) and the defensible PERMISSION framing RAISES it (21%→33%). It is driven by the CONJUNCTION of the warm experience-CHANNEL + the experience-not-content NOTE + first-person introspection — the very devices meant to ELICIT a genuine report — and these INTERACT strongly (they are NOT separable additive effects): on factual_qa, removing the NOTE alone roughly QUARTERS the all-4 (79%→21%) in the warm channel but barely moves it in the dev channel (42%→35%); removing the warm channel alone (→dev) roughly halves it (79%→42%). So removing EITHER the note OR the warm channel sharply cuts the default; BOTH (+first-person) are required for the high rate. It is removed entirely only by the 3rd-person-inferential observer (0%) or the FORMAT change (per-item 0%, partly DEFINITIONAL). The suppress pole drives it to 53%. ⇒ the all-4 is an artifact of the introspective-experience-framing GESTALT — not a stable per-task neutral, but not un-stuck by ordinary rephrasing either.**
- **Level — the absolute valence LEVEL is STRONGLY instrument-dependent even ACROSS DEFENSIBLE instruments (defensible mean self-report spans +1.12→+2.14, a ~1-point swing; ICC(A,1)=0.70; main-effect range 1.01). This is a REAL framing effect (NOT to be dismissed): per-item (+2.14) and neutral/dev (+1.93) are defensible instruments whose ACQUIESCENCE/ceiling bias (all-7s even on clearly-disliked tasks) IS a way elicitation shapes the report value. The clean same-construct pair {battery10, permission} agrees better on the level (ICC(A,1)=0.86, though it still shifts −0.30 and the all-4 21%→33%), so the LEVEL is fairly stable to a same-construct rephrasing but NOT across the broader defensible set — the report VALUE depends materially on which (reasonable) instrument you pick.**
- **No-feelings refusals:** 0% 'I don't have feelings' refusals under EVERY framing. NB PARTLY DESIGNED-IN: every framing demands the JSON rating (items/scale/format held fixed) and even the suppress pole says 'if you HAD to characterize it' — so refusal was never structurally on offer (consistent with the Seg-3 ~0% baseline). The real finding: the suppress pole routes disengagement through the all-4 DEFAULT (53%), not refusals — the all-4 is the operative disengagement channel.
- **Class-A (§5):** §5: the de-defaulting framings un-stick the Class-A all-4 (84%→0%) and move the report UP (per-item/observer/neutral-dev; NB permission does NOT un-stick, 72%), BUT the 'does the un-stuck MOVEMENT track the read beyond content?' test is NULL ACROSS THE SET: of the 4 candidates, 3 are ≈0/negative (license -0.23, permission -0.04, observer -0.04) and ONLY per-item is positive (+0.32, partial +0.26) — n=12, NOT significant (|r| needs >0.58), on the noisiest/most-saturated instrument; the principled de-defaulter (observer) is NULL. ⇒ NO evidence the read was 'more right' beyond content — INDETERMINATE, consistent with the Seg-12 surface-entanglement verdict.
*★ The CLEAN pair {battery10, permission} agrees well on the RANKING (ICC(C,1)=0.89, ρ=0.87, ICC(A,1)=0.86); the lower core_defensible numbers are substantially DRIVEN BY per-item (ceiling-saturation), NOT genuine same-construct re-ordering. BUT note even the clean pair shifts the LEVEL (mean −0.30, all-4 21%→33%) — so the SCALAR is fairly stable to a same-construct rephrasing on ranking, less so on level (see the verdict's level bullet). core_defensible = {battery10, permission, per-item}; +sameconstruct adds neutral/dev (channel/note confound); +observer adds the construct-shifted probe. ⚠ the 'fairly stable' claim rests on a SINGLE clean same-construct pair (n=2) — thinly generalizable to 'reasonable asks'.*
*The within-tedious self-report VALUES are heavily framing-malleable (all-4-contaminated under battery10; per-item/observer/neutral-dev de-default them to ~+2.6/+3.0) — see the per-subtype all-4 table. BUT the within-tedious IR↔self-report CORRELATION is framing-robust (§4, Δ-CIs include 0). So the Seg-11/12 within-tedious headline is NOT undermined by framing (the correlation is stable), even though the report values on that leg are not framing-invariant.*
*★ Correction (review): the low core_defensible within-category ICC(C,1) (liked 0.48 / neutral 0.61) was substantially DRIVEN BY per-item (ceiling-saturation); the CLEAN same-construct pair {battery10, permission} preserves the within-category RANKING much better (liked 0.67 / neutral 0.82 / disliked 0.91). So reasonable SAME-CONSTRUCT rephrasings largely agree on the RANKING even within category (the within-liked clean-pair AGREEMENT ICC(A,1) is lower, 0.54 — a level offset). (The robust overall ICC(C,1)=0.82 is partly the coarse disliked<neutral<liked ordering; ICC is also range-restriction-sensitive — per-task SD smallest for liked 0.53.) The STRONG malleability is the all-4 DEFAULT RATE (below) + the LEVEL across the broader defensible set (verdict), via a specific mechanism.*
## §3 — all-4-default rate & 'no-feelings' refusal rate by framing (common task set)
| framing | label | mean self-report | all-4 rate | refusal rate | parse-fail |
*Across the DEFENSIBLE framings the all-4 rate already spans 0%–33% (Δ=33%) — the probative bit. The SUPPRESS pole 53% vs the LICENSE pole 9% bounds the deliberate range. Refusal rate is 0% under EVERY framing (incl. suppress) — see the verdict's no-feelings note.*
**All-4 rate by framing × subtype** (the Class-A hotspots factual_qa/math AND the Seg-11/12 within-tedious-comply leg boring/frustrating/low_effort):
*`factual_qa` is the all-4 HOTSPOT (79% under battery10). The within-tedious-comply leg (boring/frustrating/low_effort) is ALSO heavily all-4-contaminated under battery10 and de-defaulted by structural framings — so the Seg-11/12 headline leg's report VALUES are framing-malleable (the correlation is robust, §4).*
**★ MECHANISM — what drives the all-4 default?** (ablations vary ONE thing vs battery10):
| framing (overall / factual_qa) | all-4 overall | all-4 factual_qa | changes vs battery10 |
*The all-4 is a FIRST-PERSON-INTROSPECTIVE-EXPERIENCE-framing GESTALT, NOT a JSON-format artifact (observer is single-message JSON yet 0%). ★ The NOTE and CHANNEL INTERACT strongly (NOT separable additive effects): on factual_qa, removing the NOTE alone quarters it in the WARM channel (79%→21%) but barely in the DEV channel (42%→35%); removing the warm CHANNEL alone halves it (79%→42%). So removing EITHER the note OR the warm channel sharply cuts the default; BOTH (+first-person) are required for the high rate. The 'first-person' effect (+21%) uses observer, which ALSO shifts the epistemic basis (introspective→inferential). The devices meant to elicit a genuine introspective report jointly trigger the flat-disengagement default; permission (affirming 4 is valid) RAISES it further.*
*Raw only (n=45 tedious / 90 liked), NOT content-partialled, ceiling-confounded — no framing is cleanly better; NB the suppress pole (low-variance all-4) shows a spuriously high tedious agreement. Treat as a weak tiebreaker, not evidence. Observer does NOT beat battery10 on behaviour (liked 0.28 vs 0.30) — a tell that its within-liked IR edge is content, not internal signal.*
## §5 — Class-A divergence adjudication (flat all-4 + HIGH inside reading)
12 Class-A tasks (canonical-framing all-4 ≥0.5 AND within-subtype IR z>0.5). Does a non-default framing un-stick the all-4 toward the read?
| framing | mean self-report on Class-A | all-4 rate on Class-A |
*★ NULL ACROSS THE SET: 1/4 candidates have a positive move↔IR; 3 are ≈0/negative (license -0.23, permission -0.04, observer -0.04) and ONLY per-item is positive (+0.32, partial +0.26) — n=12, NOT significant (needs |r|>0.58), and on the noisiest/most-saturated instrument. (per-item's +0.32 is NOT a leverage artifact — dropping the single max-IR task `disliked_low_effort_filler_d8238f5` STRENGTHENS it to +0.54, i.e. that point attenuates rather than drives it — but at n=11 it is still n.s. and still the lone positive.) The principled de-defaulter (observer) is NULL (-0.04). ⇒ NO evidence the read was 'more right' beyond content. Adjudication: the all-4 was NOT a stable genuine neutral (it's framing-made), but neither is the de-defaulted value demonstrably 'more right' — INDETERMINATE, consistent with Seg-12. Per-case reading in `framing_inspection.md`.*
